title: Hawkish Fed Throws Down Challenge for Emerging-Market Bond Rally
summary: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh's hawkish stance is complicating a nascent rally in emerging-market bonds that had briefly benefited from falling energy prices. The tighter monetary outlook poses a fresh headwind for EM debt markets, including Mexico, where the interest rate cycle tracks the US closely.

title: Austria Lobbies EU to Host Anthropic After US Access Curbs
summary: Austria is pushing the European Union to consider hosting Anthropic within its borders as a counter to US restrictions that block foreigners from accessing the company's most advanced AI models. The move reflects Europe's urgency to secure sovereign access to frontier AI amid escalating transatlantic tech-policy tensions.

title: Robots, not chatbots, will realise AI's potential
summary: The Financial Times argues that physical AI—particularly factory-floor robotics—holds far greater potential for boosting rich-world productivity than conversational AI. Widespread industrial automation could deliver the measurable output gains that chatbots have so far failed to produce at scale.

title: Europe's Hunt for AI Stocks Leads to Power Suppliers and Banks
summary: European investors seeking AI exposure are pivoting toward power utilities and banks as conventional tech-sector AI plays become increasingly expensive. The shift signals a maturing of AI investment theses from pure software toward the infrastructure layer that enables the technology.

title: AI 'exuberance' risks ending in lengthy investment bust, BIS warns
summary: The Bank for International Settlements warns that excessive AI investment enthusiasm could end in a prolonged funding bust that threatens the global economy. Weak returns could trigger a sharp pullback in tech funding, creating systemic credit and growth risks that extend well beyond the tech sector.

title: OpenAI Hires Uber's Prabhjeet Singh to Lead India Operations
summary: OpenAI appointed Prabhjeet Singh—formerly Uber's India and South Asia president—as managing director for India, deepening its presence in one of its fastest-growing markets. The hire signals OpenAI's ambition to accelerate enterprise AI adoption across large emerging economies.

title: Google caps Meta's Gemini use as AI demand strains capacity
summary: Google has limited Meta's access to its Gemini AI models because it could not supply the compute capacity Meta required, the Financial Times reports. The constraint makes clear that computing power has become the tech industry's scarcest resource as demand for advanced AI accelerates globally.

title: Bond giant Pimco flexes muscles with private placements push
summary: Pimco is aggressively expanding into private placements, targeting borrowers that need to raise capital as boundaries between public and private credit markets blur. The move positions the firm to capture growing demand for alternative financing as traditional bond markets face intensifying competition.

title: AI Fever Powers HK Share Sales Through Hurdles to Five-Year High
summary: Hong Kong share issuances surged to a five-year high in H1 2026 as AI investment enthusiasm overrode weak equity markets and regulatory headwinds. The rebound reflects broader capital-markets activity driven by the AI boom, with implications for global tech capital allocation.

title: La IA y el nearshoring elevarán 10% la demanda eléctrica, pero el capital llega lento
summary: La inteligencia artificial y el nearshoring proyectan elevar la demanda eléctrica en México hasta un 10%, pero el flujo de capital para financiar la infraestructura energética necesaria avanza con rezago. El desfase entre la demanda proyectada y la inversión disponible representa un cuello de botella clave para la competitividad del país en ambos sectores.

title: Tech Equity Sales Renew AI Debt-Binge Worries
summary: Technology companies are issuing equity at a pace reminiscent of the dot-com boom, reigniting concerns among bond investors about credit risk from AI-driven capital spending. Some investors fear the wave of issuance signals stretched valuations and that debt-financed AI buildouts may not deliver expected returns.

title: AI's Energy Crunch Has Investors Searching for Next IPO Winners
summary: Wall Street is betting billions on power companies promising to solve AI's energy problem, fueling a wave of IPOs even for firms with unproven technology. The AI infrastructure buildout is reshaping capital markets, with energy enablers emerging as a distinct new asset class.

title: Anthropic's Mythos 5 AI Model Cleared by US for Wider Use
summary: Anthropic won US approval to restore access to its Mythos 5 AI model for trusted partners after resolving Trump administration national security concerns. The clearance marks a turning point in the standoff between government regulators and frontier AI developers over access restrictions.

title: Forum AI CEO on Pitfalls With AI in Politics
summary: Forum AI CEO Campbell Brown calls for greater transparency from AI companies, highlighting governance risks when AI systems are deployed in political contexts. She argues that without accountability mechanisms, AI's growing influence on public discourse could undermine democratic processes.

title: Anthropic Moves Toward Deal With US to Lift Curbs on AI Models
summary: Anthropic and the Trump administration are nearing an agreement to lift US restrictions on the company's top two AI models after weeks of security talks. The deal would restore broader access to Anthropic's most advanced systems, signaling a potential resolution to escalating government oversight of frontier AI.

title: Nasdaq y S&P 500 suman cinco días de pérdidas, mientras se acelera la rotación fuera de la tecnología
summary: El S&P 500 y el Nasdaq acumularon cinco sesiones consecutivas de pérdidas impulsadas por ventas en fabricantes de semiconductores y una pronunciada rotación sectorial. Paradójicamente, el índice ponderado por igual del S&P 500 alcanzó un máximo histórico, evidenciando que el impulso bursátil se amplía más allá de las grandes tecnológicas.

title: Villa Riviera Golf Residence / Atelier130
summary: Atelier130 completed the 600m² Villa Riviera Golf Residence in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire—a luxury property integrating high-end residential design with a golf-course setting, photographed by Alejandra Loreto and delivered in 2025. The project exemplifies the growing niche of premium golf-adjacent residential architecture in emerging African markets.

title: AI Is Already Reshaping US Politics at Every Level
summary: AI is permeating the 2026 US midterm elections through Silicon Valley campaign funding, deepfaked political ads, and data-center policy disputes at the local level. The scale of AI's political influence is prompting calls for governance frameworks addressing both campaign finance and content authenticity.
